summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to discuss potential text amendments to the Goshen Township Zoning Code. Key discussions focused on allowing barns and storage structures as primary structures on R1 lots without a residence, limiting the number of accessory structures and lot coverage percentages on unimproved lots, clarifying rules for accessory structures on adjoining lots under common ownership, and updating the agricultural definition to match state language. The board reached consensus on several zoning text amendment provisions and plans to forward the finalized language for formal resolution and zoning commission review.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Consensus reached to allow barns/storage structures as primary use in R1 district on lots 2+ acres with maximum 2 buildings and 10% lot coverage when no residence is present
Consensus reached to retain existing lot coverage percentages (no number limit on structures) when a principal residence exists on the property
Consensus to remove the second sentence in 701B3 (starting with 'Additionally existing properties may not be subdivided...') and add 'adjoining' language for clarity
Consensus to change 'subordinate' to 'incidental' in B2 for consistency with accessory structure definition
Consensus on agricultural definition using state language
Consensus on Zoning Inspector authority for administrative relief (Section 206)
Motion to adjourn at 7:54 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Consultant Liz Fields to finalize zoning text amendment language based on board direction from this meeting
Prosecutor Carney to prepare a resolution for adoption and initiation of the text amendments, similar to the previous week's resolution
Finalized amendments to be sent to Zoning Commission and Planning Commission for review
Staff to present the remaining list of zoning issues to address at the next trustee meeting
Board to identify the next three zoning items for future amendment work and forward to consultants
Melissa (Zoning Inspector) to identify common variance requests and recurring code issues to help prioritize future amendments
key_amounts: 50% maximum lot coverage currently allowed for accessory structures on lots 2+ acres
10% lot coverage agreed upon for unimproved R1 lots (no residence) with barn/storage as primary structure
15% lot coverage initially discussed but reduced to 10% (~8,000+ sq ft on 2 acres)
75-foot setback required for barns as primary structures from the road
7% maximum size of total lot area for principal barn/storage structure
2 acres minimum lot size required for barn as primary structure in R1
notable_topics: Allowing barns and storage structures as primary/principal use on R1 lots without a residence
Number of accessory structures permitted on lots without a principal residence
Lot coverage percentage limits for unimproved lots vs. lots with residences
Accessory structures on adjoining lots under common ownership (Section 701B3)
Zoning Inspector authority for administrative relief (Section 206)
Agricultural definition update to match Ohio state definition
Setback considerations for future house construction when barn is primary structure
Process for text amendments through Zoning Commission and Planning Commission
Prioritizing next set of zoning code amendments including fences and corner lots
attendees: Dan (Trustee Allen, referenced by first name)
notes: This was a working session-style special meeting focused on detailed zoning text amendment language. The board engaged in extensive deliberation about balancing property owner flexibility with preventing lot clutter. A previous zoning director had helped develop the initial list of zoning issues to address. The board noted they have completed approximately 6-8 items from their collaborative list. The board discussed a historical Goshen Township rule requiring 5-acre minimum lots for properties more than 300 feet from road frontage, which appears to have been removed from the code. The amendments discussed will need to go through the formal process of Zoning Commission and Planning Commission review before returning to the trustees for final approval.
title: BOT Special Meeting 6-22-23
